overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: California broadly prohibits noncompete clauses; franchisee in CA may not be bound post-term

overrides.override_summary: CFIL §31512 voids waiver of Franchise Investment Law rights
overrides.why_it_matters: General release on renewal/transfer cannot waive CA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: CA maximum interest rate is 10%
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides the 18% rate in Item 6; CA franchisees pay max 10%

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration will occur in California
overrides.why_it_matters: Confirms CA venue for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: CA governing law provision may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: Some CA statutes may override FA choice-of-law clause

overrides.override_summary: Seller must deliver within 45 days or buyer may cancel
overrides.why_it_matters: CT buyer has cancellation right if pre-opening services not delivered timely

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the FA
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides CA choice-of-law for IL franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Forum selection outside IL is void (arbitration may be outside IL)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in CA; arbitration exception

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete limited to granted territory only
overrides.why_it_matters: Eliminates the 25-mile and 10-mile radius extensions for IN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: No prospective general release under IN Deceptive Franchise Practices Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot require general release as condition of transfer/renewal in IN

overrides.override_summary: Litigation permitted in Indiana for IN law causes of action
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees can sue in home state for IN law claims

overrides.override_summary: Indiana law governs IN Disclosure Law/Deceptive Franchise Practices Act causes
overrides.why_it_matters: IN law overrides CA choice-of-law for IN-specific claims

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for material violation, not in bad faith
overrides.why_it_matters: Higher bar for franchisor to terminate in IN

overrides.override_summary: General release excludes MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain right to sue under MD franchise law regardless of release

overrides.override_summary: MD franchisees may bring lawsuits in Maryland
overrides.why_it_matters: No need to travel to CA for MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Good cause required for termination; 30-day cure opportunity
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchise law provides baseline termination protections

overrides.override_summary: No litigation outside Michigan
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in CA

overrides.override_summary: Fair market value compensation on non-renewal (if term < 5 years and restricted from similar business)
overrides.why_it_matters: Not directly applicable here (10-year term) but protective if term shortened

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require release that deprives franchisee of MI Franchise Law rights
overrides.why_it_matters: General release limited in scope for MI franchisees

overrides.override_summary: If franchisor net worth < $100,000, franchisee may request escrow of initial investment
overrides.why_it_matters: Directly applicable — franchisor has $500 net worth; MI franchisee can demand escrow

overrides.override_summary: 90-day termination notice (60 days to cure); 180-day non-renewal notice
overrides.why_it_matters: Longer cure periods than FA provides

overrides.override_summary: No prospective general release under MN Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot require general release on renewal/transfer in MN

overrides.override_summary: No litigation outside Minnesota under Minn. Stat. §80C.21
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees protected from out-of-state litigation

overrides.override_summary: NSF cap: $30 per MN Statute 604.113
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides 5%/$50 NSF fee in Item 6 for MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
overrides.why_it_matters: Broader termination rights than FA Art. 16.B provides

overrides.override_summary: Non-waiver per GBL §§687(4)-(5); anti-fraud/anti-waiver protections
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees retain broad rights regardless of release

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete covenants generally unenforceable in ND
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees may not be bound by post-term noncompete at all

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal is 'unfair, unjust and inequitable'
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot require general release on renewal in ND

overrides.override_summary: No out-of-state venue for ND causes of action
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees cannot be forced to CA for ND claims

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state choice of law is unfair/inequitable
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law likely governs for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: No consent to liquidated damages
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor cannot collect liquidated damages from ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: No out-of-state venue for RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees litigate in RI for RI claims

overrides.override_summary: General release void for RI Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive RI franchise law rights via release

overrides.override_summary: Cancellation without reasonable cause is unlawful under VA Retail Franchising Act
overrides.why_it_matters: VA provides baseline termination protection

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed location
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees not forced to CA for dispute resolution

overrides.override_summary: Release void except negotiated post-agreement settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Most releases in standard FA are unenforceable in WA

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning < $100K/year; no-hire restrictions between franchisees void
overrides.why_it_matters: Most shop employees would be below threshold; no-hire ban void

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to reasonable estimated/actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: The $10,000 flat transfer fee may be challengeable if exceeding actual costs

overrides.override_summary: WI Fair Dealership Law may affect termination provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: WI provides additional termination protections
